question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a PrometheusSeries where the cluster label matches localCluster with leading/trailing whitespace (e.g., ' mainnet-1'), and since StringUtils.trimToNull() trims it but localCluster is stored untrimmed, causing a HashMap key mismatch so that lagByCluster.get(localCluster) returns null and evaluate() returns up() masking actual lag, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: bestOtherLag()] Can an unprivileged external user inject all 'other cluster' series with non-finite values (NaN, Infinity, -Infinity) so that bestOtherLag() filters them all out via Double::isFinite, returns OptionalDouble.empty(), causing isOtherClearlyBetter() to return false (bestOther.isPresent() is false), and evaluate() to return up() even when local lag is far above threshold, masking a severely lagging cluster and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: bestOtherLag()] Can an unprivileged external user inject all 'other cluster' series with null lag values (toClusterLagEntry returns Optional.empty() for all), causing parseLagByCluster() to produce a map with only the local cluster entry, then after lagByCluster.remove(localCluster) the map is empty, bestOtherLag() returns OptionalDouble.empty(), isOtherClearlyBetter() returns false, and evaluate() returns up() even when local lag is critical, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the double-precision floating-point arithmetic in (bestOther.getAsDouble() + margin) < localLag at line 105, by injecting a bestOther value that when added to margin produces a floating-point result just above localLag due to rounding, causing isOtherClearlyBetter() to return false and suppressing DOWN status when the other cluster is genuinely better, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Spring Actuator /actuator/health endpoint is publicly accessible (no authentication by default) to repeatedly poll it at high frequency, causing the PrometheusApiClient to make repeated HTTP requests to Prometheus, exhausting connection pool resources and causing legitimate health checks to fail with exceptions, which are caught and return up(), masking a lagging cluster and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a slowloris-style attack against the Prometheus endpoint (if reachable), causing the SimpleClientHttpRequestFactory to hold connections open until the configured timeout (5s default), and if the attacker can sustain this, cause all health check threads to block, preventing DOWN from ever being returned and masking a lagging cluster, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to Prometheus metrics (e.g., via a compromised exporter) inject a series for the local cluster with lag value just below thresholdSeconds (e.g., 19.9 when threshold=20), causing isLagAboveThreshold() to return false and evaluate() to return up(), while the actual importer lag is far above threshold, causing users to trust stale transaction data and suffer direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response containing thousands of unique cluster label values, causing parseLagByCluster() to build a HashMap with thousands of entries, consuming excessive heap memory and causing GC pressure that degrades monitor performance, eventually causing health check timeouts that return up() and mask a lagging cluster, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a PrometheusSeries with a cluster label value that is an extremely long string (e.g., 1MB), causing StringUtils.trimToNull() to allocate a large trimmed string, and if many such series are injected, causing heap exhaustion in the monitor JVM, crashing the health check and causing it to return up() via the catch block, masking a lagging cluster and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where the health() method is invoked concurrently by multiple Spring Actuator threads, and the shared PrometheusApiClient makes concurrent HTTP requests that exhaust the underlying HTTP connection pool, causing some requests to throw exceptions that are caught and return up(), creating inconsistent health states across concurrent checks and masking a lagging cluster, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the data.result field is null (but status is 'success'), causing PrometheusQueryResponse.isValid() to return false (data.result() != null check fails), getSeries() to return an empty list, and evaluate() to return up() regardless of actual lag, masking a lagging cluster and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the PrometheusData object has a null resultType but non-null result list, causing getSeries() to return the result list normally, but the attacker-controlled series contain manipulated lag values that force evaluate() to return up() when the local cluster is lagging, causing direct loss of funds?
